brazil.note: Brazil is Latin America's largest e-commerce market, and many retailers and streaming services geo-restrict catalogs and pricing to Brazilian IPs, so local residential IPs are needed for accurate data.

argentina.note: Argentina's currency controls and frequent price volatility mean local IPs are important for capturing accurate peso pricing that differs sharply from prices shown to foreign visitors.

colombia.note: Colombia is one of the fastest-growing e-commerce markets in the Andean region, and retailers like Exito and Falabella localize promotions by region, so Colombian IPs are used for market-price monitoring.

chile.note: Chile has the highest internet penetration in South America and mature online retail, so Chilean IPs are commonly used to track competitive pricing across Falabella, Ripley and Paris.

peru.note: Peru's online retail is concentrated in Lima and dominated by Falabella and Ripley, and local IPs help capture region-specific shipping and pricing that vary from foreign views.

venezuela.note: Venezuela has heavy state internet controls and periodic blocking of sites, so local IPs are used to reach content and pricing that is otherwise filtered or geo-restricted for outside visitors.

ecuador.note: Ecuador uses the US dollar, and while pricing is stable, local IPs are still needed to access region-locked promotions and classifieds listings restricted to Ecuadorian visitors.

bolivia.note: Bolivia has lower e-commerce penetration and much retail happens via classifieds and social marketplaces, so local IPs are used mainly for accessing region-restricted listings and ad content.

paraguay.note: Paraguay is a smaller bilingual market where much commerce runs through classifieds like Clasipar, and local IPs are needed to view listings and offers geo-limited to Paraguayan users.

uruguay.note: Uruguay's fixed broadband is dominated by the state operator Antel, and Uruguayan IPs are used to access local classifieds like Gallito and region-restricted retail pricing.

guyana.note: Guyana is a small English-speaking market with a fast-growing oil economy, and local IPs are used to reach region-restricted classifieds and news content limited to Guyanese visitors.

suriname.note: Suriname is a Dutch-speaking market with fixed and mobile service dominated by state operator Telesur, and local IPs help access region-limited local media and classifieds.

nigeria.note: Nigeria is Africa's largest e-commerce and mobile market, and platforms like Jumia and Jiji localize by region, so Nigerian IPs are essential for accurate price and listing data.

south-africa.note: South Africa is the continent's most developed online retail market led by Takealot, and many services geo-restrict streaming and pricing, so local IPs are needed for market data.

egypt.note: Egypt is the largest e-commerce market in North Africa, and retailers geo-price in Egyptian pounds while some content is filtered, so local IPs are used for accurate pricing and access.

kenya.note: Kenya's e-commerce is tightly linked to Safaricom's M-Pesa mobile money, and many checkout and pricing flows only work correctly from Kenyan IPs.

ghana.note: Ghana has a rapidly growing mobile-first internet economy dominated by MTN, and local IPs are used to access region-restricted classifieds and localized retail pricing.

morocco.note: Morocco's online market runs heavily through classifieds like Avito, and content is often bilingual, so local IPs are used to view listings and offers geo-limited to Moroccan users.

algeria.note: Algeria's online commerce centers on the classifieds site Ouedkniss, and periodic internet restrictions make Algerian IPs useful for reaching local listings and content reliably.

tunisia.note: Tunisia's e-commerce leans on classifieds like Tayara, and local bilingual content and pricing are best captured with Tunisian IPs.

ethiopia.note: Ethiopia's telecom sector was long a state monopoly under Ethio Telecom and has seen periodic internet shutdowns, so local IPs are used to reach region-restricted listings and content.

tanzania.note: Tanzania is a fast-growing mobile-money market where much retail runs through classifieds, and local IPs help access region-limited listings and Swahili-language content.

uganda.note: Uganda has occasionally imposed social media and internet taxes and shutdowns, so Ugandan IPs are used to reliably reach local classifieds and geo-restricted content.

angola.note: Angola is a Portuguese-speaking market dominated by mobile operator Unitel, and local IPs are used to access region-restricted classifieds and localized pricing in kwanza.

cameroon.note: Cameroon is a bilingual market where much commerce runs through classifieds like CoinAfrique, and local IPs are used to view listings and content geo-limited to Cameroonian users.

senegal.note: Sonatel-owned Orange dominates connectivity, so local Senegalese IPs are needed to reach classifieds like Expat-Dakar and price data that is served in French for the Dakar market.

ivory-coast.note: Abidjan is a major francophone West African e-commerce hub where mobile-money-driven retail on Jumia and CoinAfrique is best accessed through Ivorian mobile IPs.

zimbabwe.note: Zimbabwe has high mobile-data costs and Econet dominance, so mobile proxies on local carrier IPs are common for accessing classifieds and news portals restricted or throttled abroad.

zambia.note: Zambia's online market is small and English-language, and local IPs help reach news and classifieds sites that occasionally geo-limit traffic to the Copperbelt and Lusaka regions.

mozambique.note: Mozambique is a Portuguese-language market where Movitel has strong rural coverage, so local mobile IPs are useful for reaching content served regionally in Portuguese.

rwanda.note: Rwanda pushes a cashless digital economy centered on Kigali, and local IPs are needed for accurate access to government and news portals that serve content in Kinyarwanda and English.

botswana.note: Botswana is a small, sparsely populated market where Mascom leads mobile, so local IPs matter for reaching the handful of national news and classifieds sites.

namibia.note: MTC holds most of Namibia's mobile market, and Namibian IPs are needed to reach national news outlets and price data serving the small English-speaking population.

democratic-republic-of-the-congo.note: The DRC is a large but low-connectivity francophone market where access is heavily mobile, making local carrier IPs important for reaching Kinshasa news and classifieds.

libya.note: Libya's fragmented infrastructure and intermittent connectivity mean local IPs are often the only reliable way to reach Arabic-language news sites serving the domestic audience.

sudan.note: Sudan has faced repeated government internet shutdowns, so local IPs are used to reach Arabic news portals and to monitor connectivity from inside the country.

united-arab-emirates.note: The UAE blocks most VoIP calling and many streaming and gambling sites at the ISP level, so local Emirati IPs are essential for testing what real users actually see.

saudi-arabia.note: Saudi Arabia is the Gulf's largest e-commerce market with heavy Noon and Amazon.sa usage, and local IPs are needed for accurate SAR pricing and to bypass content filtering.

israel.note: Israeli classifieds and price-comparison sites like Yad2 and Zap serve Hebrew content and geo-limit some data, so local Israeli IPs are needed for accurate market research.

turkey.note: Turkey is a large e-commerce market where Trendyol and Hepsiburada geo-price in lira and Sahibinden aggressively blocks foreign IPs, making local Turkish IPs essential for scraping.

qatar.note: Qatar is a high-income Gulf market with only two operators, and local IPs are needed to reach classifieds like Qatar Living and delivery apps that restrict access by region.

kuwait.note: Kuwait's 4Sale dominates local classifieds and, like many Gulf platforms, restricts access to Kuwaiti IPs, so local addresses are required for reliable scraping.

bahrain.note: Bahrain is a small but highly connected Gulf market where local IPs help reach classifieds and delivery services that price and serve content specifically for the island.

oman.note: Oman filters VoIP and some content at the ISP level, so local Omani IPs are needed to test real user access and to reach OpenSooq listings priced in rials.

jordan.note: Jordan is the regional headquarters for OpenSooq, and local IPs are needed to reach Arabic classifieds and news portals that tailor content to the Jordanian market.

lebanon.note: Lebanon's telecom is state-linked with frequent outages, so local IPs are used to reach classifieds and delivery apps serving the Beirut market despite unstable infrastructure.

iraq.note: Iraq experiences scheduled internet shutdowns during exams and unrest, and local IPs are needed to reach Miswag e-commerce and OpenSooq classifieds serving the domestic market.

iran.note: Iran runs one of the world's most restrictive internet filters and sits under sanctions, so most foreign platforms block Iranian IPs while local sites like Digikala require them.

yemen.note: Yemen's conflict has split telecom control and caused frequent outages, so local IPs are used mainly to reach and monitor Arabic news sites serving the domestic audience.

united-states.note: The US is the world's largest e-commerce market where retailers geo-price by state and ZIP, so residential and city-level IPs are essential for accurate pricing and ad verification.

canada.note: Canada is bilingual and retailers commonly geo-price and localize between English and French (Quebec) markets, so Canadian IPs from the right province matter for accurate data.

mexico.note: Mexico is Latin America's second-largest e-commerce market and MercadoLibre dominates local pricing, so Mexican residential IPs are used heavily for retail and marketplace research.

guatemala.note: Guatemala's mobile market is a Tigo/Claro duopoly and Encuentra24 is the main regional classifieds site, making local IPs useful for ad verification and listings scraping.

honduras.note: Honduras has a small Tigo/Claro-led telecom market, and local classifieds and news sites are common targets, so residential IPs help with geo-accurate ad and listing checks.

el-salvador.note: El Salvador made Bitcoin legal tender in 2021, drawing fintech and crypto research traffic where local IPs matter for testing region-specific services.

nicaragua.note: Nicaragua has a concentrated Claro/Tigo market and state pressure on independent media, so local IPs are used to verify how sites and ads appear in-country.

costa-rica.note: Costa Rica has one of Central America's highest internet penetration rates, and Kolbi (state ICE) leads mobile, so local IPs are valued for stable regional research.

panama.note: Panama is a regional banking and logistics hub, and Encuentra24 dominates local classifieds, making Panamanian IPs useful for finance and marketplace verification.

dominican-republic.note: The Dominican Republic is a large Caribbean e-commerce and tourism market where Corotos leads classifieds, so local IPs help with listings and travel-price research.

jamaica.note: Jamaica's telecom is a Digicel/Flow duopoly, and English-language content plus a large diaspora make local IPs useful for media and ad verification.

cuba.note: Cuba's internet is a state monopoly under ETECSA with heavy restrictions and limited access, so genuine in-country IPs are rare and hard to source.

trinidad-and-tobago.note: Trinidad and Tobago is the wealthiest English-speaking Caribbean market, and local IPs are used to verify how regional media and ads render in-country.

the-bahamas.note: The Bahamas is a tourism-driven market with a BTC/Aliv mobile duopoly, so local IPs help verify travel pricing and region-locked content.

australia.note: Australia is a high-value English-speaking e-commerce market with strong geo-targeting, so Australian IPs are essential for accurate retail, classifieds, and ad data.

new-zealand.note: New Zealand's dominant marketplace Trade Me and many services restrict access to local IPs, making NZ residential IPs important for listings and pricing checks.

papua-new-guinea.language: English, Tok Pisin and Hiri Motu
papua-new-guinea.note: Papua New Guinea has low internet penetration led by Digicel, so genuine local IPs are scarce and mainly used for media and telecom research.

fiji.note: Fiji is the main Pacific island hub with a Vodafone/Digicel market, and local IPs are used to verify tourism pricing and regional media.

solomon-islands.note: Solomon Islands has very limited connectivity dominated by Our Telekom, so in-country IPs are rare and used mainly for local media access.

vanuatu.note: Vanuatu is a small Pacific market with a TVL/Digicel duopoly, and local IPs are used for offshore-finance and tourism verification.

samoa.note: Samoa is a small Pacific island market served by Digicel and Vodafone, so genuine local IPs are limited and used mainly for local media checks.

china.note: China's Great Firewall blocks most Western sites and heavily filters traffic, so mainland IPs behave very differently and local platforms like Taobao are the main targets.

japan.note: Japan is one of the world's largest e-commerce markets with strong local platforms like Rakuten and Mercari, so Japanese IPs are essential for pricing and marketplace data.

south-korea.note: South Korea has among the fastest broadband and a Naver/Coupang-led ecosystem that heavily favors local IPs, making Korean residential IPs important for search and retail research.

north-korea.note: North Korea has no open public internet, only a state-controlled intranet (Kwangmyong), so genuine residential IPs are effectively unavailable.

taiwan.note: Taiwan is a dense e-commerce market led by PChome, momo, and Shopee, and uses Traditional Chinese, so local IPs are needed for accurate pricing and localization checks.

hong-kong.note: Hong Kong is a major financial and data-center hub with abundant clean IP space, making it a common exit point for scraping regional e-commerce and financial data.

macau.note: Macau is a small market dominated by CTM, and its gaming and tourism sites often geo-restrict content, so local Macau IPs are needed for accurate access.

mongolia.note: Mongolia is a small internet market where Unegui.mn dominates classifieds, and local IPs are needed to access Mongolian-language listings and pricing.

india.note: India is one of the world's largest e-commerce markets with heavy region-based pricing and app-only deals, so local Indian IPs are essential for accurate retail and travel data.

pakistan.note: Pakistan's online retail is led by Daraz and classifieds by OLX and PakWheels, and local IPs are needed as some sites and government portals restrict foreign access.

bangladesh.note: Bangladesh has a fast-growing e-commerce market led by Daraz and classifieds by Bikroy, and local IPs help access Bangla listings and ride-hailing pricing.

sri-lanka.note: Sri Lanka's classifieds are dominated by ikman.lk and retail by Daraz.lk, and local IPs are needed to view accurate rupee pricing and local listings.

nepal.note: Nepal's online shopping is led by Daraz and classifieds by Hamrobazar, and local IPs are needed to access Nepali-language marketplaces and delivery pricing.

bhutan.note: Bhutan is a very small internet market served mainly by Bhutan Telecom and TashiCell, so residential IPs are scarce and mostly tied to those two operators.

maldives.note: The Maldives is a small market split between Dhiraagu and Ooredoo, and its tourism and news portals often serve region-specific content requiring local IPs.

afghanistan.note: Afghanistan has limited and unstable internet infrastructure, and local IPs are hard to source, with mobile carriers providing most connectivity.

indonesia.note: Indonesia is Southeast Asia's largest e-commerce market with heavy flash-sale and app-based pricing, so local IPs are essential for accurate Tokopedia and Shopee data.

vietnam.note: Vietnam is a fast-growing market where Shopee, Tiki and Chotot dominate, and local IPs are needed as some content is filtered and pricing is region-specific.

thailand.note: Thailand has a mature e-commerce market led by Shopee and Lazada, and local IPs are needed to view Thai-baht pricing and access geo-restricted content.

philippines.note: The Philippines is a major mobile-first e-commerce market where Shopee and Lazada lead, and local IPs are essential for accurate peso pricing and app promotions.

malaysia.note: Malaysia's e-commerce is led by Shopee and Lazada with classifieds on Mudah.my, and local IPs are needed to access ringgit pricing and local-only deals.

singapore.note: Singapore is a top-tier data-center hub with excellent connectivity, widely used as a clean, low-latency exit point for scraping across Southeast Asia.

myanmar.note: Myanmar has heavy internet restrictions and frequent shutdowns, and local IPs are difficult to source with the state-linked MPT dominating connectivity.

cambodia.note: Cambodia's classifieds are dominated by Khmer24 and connectivity is largely mobile-based, so local IPs are needed to access Khmer-language listings.

laos.note: Laos is a small internet market with limited residential IP availability, dominated by state-linked Lao Telecom and Unitel.

brunei.note: Brunei is a very small market where all broadband runs over the state UNN backbone, so residential IPs are scarce and concentrated among a few carriers.

timor-leste.note: Timor-Leste has one of Asia's least-developed internet markets with sparse residential IP space, mostly served through mobile carriers.

kazakhstan.note: Kazakhstan's digital economy is dominated by the Kaspi.kz super-app, and local IPs are needed as the country operates internet monitoring and occasional restrictions.

uzbekistan.note: Uzbekistan's online market is growing around Uzum and OLX.uz, and local IPs are needed as international bandwidth is state-controlled and some sites are filtered.

turkmenistan.note: Turkmenistan has one of the world's most restricted and heavily censored internets with almost no open residential IP availability, all routed through the state Turkmentelecom.

kyrgyzstan.note: Kyrgyzstan is a small internet market where Russian is widely used online, so local IPs are mainly needed for regional classifieds and pricing checks.

tajikistan.note: Tajikistan has heavy state internet control and frequently blocks social media and messaging apps, so residential IP availability is limited.

united-kingdom.note: The UK is a large e-commerce market and streaming services like BBC iPlayer and ITVX restrict content to UK IPs, so local IPs are essential for accurate access.

germany.note: Germany enforces strict GDPR data rules and many retailers geo-price by region, so local German IPs are essential for accurate market data.

france.note: France is a top-five EU e-commerce market and Leboncoin classifieds and local price checks commonly require French residential IPs.

italy.note: Italy's largest classifieds site Subito.it and regional retail pricing are commonly targeted, and local IPs help avoid access blocks.

spain.note: Wallapop and Idealista are the key local marketplaces, and Spanish IPs are needed to see regional pricing and listings accurately.

poland.note: Allegro dominates Polish e-commerce, making local IPs important for accurate pricing and listing data.

ireland.note: Ireland shares much retail with the UK, but property site Daft.ie and DoneDeal classifieds are the main local targets requiring Irish IPs.

netherlands.note: Bol.com and Marktplaats dominate the Dutch market, so local IPs are needed for accurate pricing and marketplace data.

belgium.note: Belgium is a trilingual market where site content varies between Flemish and Walloon regions, so language and region targeting matter.

portugal.note: OLX.pt classifieds and retailers Worten and Continente are the main local targets requiring Portuguese IPs.

switzerland.note: Switzerland is a high-value market where Ricardo and Tutti are the main local marketplaces and content differs across three language regions.

austria.note: Willhaben is the dominant Austrian classifieds and marketplace site, and Austrian IPs are needed to separate its data from the German market.

sweden.note: Blocket classifieds and Prisjakt price comparison are key targets, and Swedish IPs are needed to view accurate local listings.

norway.note: Finn.no is the dominant Norwegian classifieds marketplace and typically requires local IPs for full access.

denmark.note: DBA.dk classifieds and PriceRunner comparison are common targets requiring Danish IPs for accurate local data.

finland.note: Tori.fi and Verkkokauppa.com are the main local e-commerce sites, and Finnish IPs are needed to view accurate regional listings.

iceland.note: Iceland is a very small market with limited local IP pools, so residential proxy coverage there is scarce and often expensive.

estonia.note: Estonia is a highly digital society with strong e-government, and marketplaces like Osta.ee are the main local targets.

latvia.note: SS.lv (SS.com) is the dominant Latvian classifieds portal and typically needs local IPs for full listing access.

lithuania.note: Skelbiu.lt and Pigu.lt are the primary local marketplaces, and Lithuanian IPs are needed for accurate pricing and listings.

czech-republic.note: Alza.cz and the Heureka price comparison engine dominate Czech e-commerce, making local IPs important for pricing research.

slovakia.note: Bazos.sk classifieds and Alza.sk retail are common targets requiring Slovak IPs to separate them from the neighboring Czech market.

hungary.note: eMAG and the Arukereso price comparison site plus Jofogas classifieds are the key local targets requiring Hungarian IPs.

romania.note: eMAG dominates Romanian e-commerce and OLX leads classifieds, so local IPs are needed to see accurate pricing and listing availability across the country.

bulgaria.note: Bulgaria is a smaller EU market where eMAG and OLX dominate, and Cyrillic-language listings often differ from what non-local IPs are served.

greece.note: Skroutz is the dominant Greek price-comparison marketplace, making Greek residential IPs important for tracking retail pricing and stock.

croatia.note: Njuskalo is the leading Croatian classifieds site and enforces regional access controls, so local IPs help avoid blocks when monitoring listings.

slovenia.note: Slovenia is a compact EU market where Bolha classifieds and Mimovrste retail lead, and Slovenian IPs are needed for locale-specific pricing.

serbia.note: KupujemProdajem is Serbia's dominant classifieds platform and Serbia sits outside the EU, so local IPs matter for both access and accurate market data.

bosnia-and-herzegovina.note: OLX.ba is the country's dominant online marketplace, and the three telecoms are split along entity lines, so local IPs are useful for consistent access.

montenegro.note: Montenegro is a very small market of roughly 620,000 people, so local IP pools are limited and news portals are the main scrape targets.

north-macedonia.note: North Macedonia has effectively two mobile network operators (Makedonski Telekom and A1), and Pazar3 is the main local classifieds target.

albania.note: Gjirafa is Albania's leading local search and e-commerce platform, and the mobile market consolidated to two operators (Vodafone and One) after mergers.

kosovo.note: Kosovo lacks its own ISO country calling code standard historically and has limited IP allocation, so dedicated Kosovo IPs are relatively scarce.

ukraine.note: Rozetka is Ukraine's largest online retailer and OLX leads classifieds; wartime disruptions make IP availability and routing less stable than in the EU.

belarus.note: Onliner and Kufar dominate Belarusian e-commerce and classifieds, but international sanctions and state control complicate access from foreign IPs.

moldova.note: 999.md is by far the dominant Moldovan marketplace, so local IPs are essential for monitoring its listings and regional pricing.

luxembourg.note: Luxembourg is a small, wealthy, trilingual market where content is served in French, German or Luxembourgish, so local IPs help capture the right locale.

malta.note: English is widely used and Maltapark leads local classifieds; Malta is also a major iGaming hub, driving demand for local IP verification.

cyprus.note: Bazaraki is the dominant Cypriot classifieds site, and the island's political division means IP geolocation can differ between the north and south.

andorra.note: Andorra has a single state telecom monopoly (Andorra Telecom), so residential and mobile IPs all originate from one operator and pools are very small.

monaco.note: Monaco Telecom is effectively the sole operator in this tiny high-wealth city-state, so dedicated Monaco IPs are rare and often shared with French ranges.

liechtenstein.note: Liechtenstein uses the Swiss franc and shares mobile infrastructure with Swiss operators, so its IP space is minimal and often overlaps with Switzerland.

san-marino.note: San Marino is an enclave fully surrounded by Italy and relies on Italian telecom infrastructure, so genuine San Marino IPs are extremely limited.
